claims.claim: In a controlled experiment using a synthetic order-2 Markov teacher (32-token vocabulary) and an n-gram student, we compare five draft-construction policies across low-, mixed-, and high-entropy next-token distributions.

claims.claim: A *spine-preserving* tree policy—which maintains a deep top-1 draft chain and allocates remaining budget to high-probability side branches—yields a macro-average improvement of 7.40% in accepted greedy tokens per verification call over a top-1 chain baseline and reduces zero-accept rounds by 11.71 percentage points.

claims.claim: We propose and evaluate a *spine-preserving tree* policy: the student first builds a top-1 chain to maximum depth, then spends remaining node budget on high-probability side branches at positions where the student's confidence is low or the distribution is flat.
